summary: Russian forces launched an Iskander-M ballistic missile and 90 drones across multiple regions, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Ukrainian defenses reported numerous drone interceptions and continued strikes on Russian logistics sites, including oil depots and gas stations. International financial support continued with a €3.2 billion EU tranche for Ukraine.
key_points: Russian forces fired an Iskander-M ballistic missile and 90 drones, injuring civilians in Sumy, Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv regions (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Ukrainian drones intercepted several enemy drones over Kyiv and Crimea during the night (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
A gas station in Zaporizhzhia was hit by a Russian strike, causing a fire; similar attacks occurred in Sumy and the Nikopol district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31154).
Ukrainian forces struck a Russian oil depot in the Poltava region, igniting a fire (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukraine received a first EU tranche of €3.2 billion for reconstruction and drone procurement (Source: @UkraineNow/82171).
French authorities seized the oil tanker Deliver in the Mediterranean, alleging violation of sanctions (Source: @UkraineNow/82172).
Ukrainian officials warned of potential power outages if Russian strikes on energy infrastructure resume (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31162).
High alert issued for possible Iskander-M launches from Bryansk region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68037).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces launched an Iskander-M ballistic missile and 90 drones, causing civilian injuries and fires at gas stations in Sumy, Zaporizhzhia and the Nikopol district. Ukrainian air defenses shot down multiple enemy drones over Kyiv and Crimea, while Ukrainian UAVs struck a Russian oil depot in Poltava. The EU delivered a €3.2 billion financial tranche to support Ukraine’s reconstruction and drone procurement.

points: Ukrainian air defenses downed several enemy drones over the capital Kyiv and in Crimea during the night, indicating heightened aerial activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian UAVs attacked a Russian oil depot in the Poltava region, igniting a fire and disrupting logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).

Ukrainian officials warned that renewed large‑scale Russian strikes on the energy sector could trigger power outages, though the situation is currently better than a year ago (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31162).
Ukrainian forces reported successful defensive actions on the Sumy front, driving out enemy groups from Ivolzhanskoe and liberating settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Russian forces launched an Iskander‑M ballistic missile and 90 drones, causing injuries to four people in Sumy and damage to a gas station in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Russian strikes hit gas stations in Zaporizhzhia, Sumy and the Nikopol district, resulting in fires and civilian casualties (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31154).
A Russian strike hit a gas distribution substation in the Zaporozhye region, causing infrastructure damage (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Russian assault aircraft of the North airborne division engaged Ukrainian forces on the Sumy front, reporting skirmishes in Bachevsk and surrounding villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian forces reported drone interceptions and artillery engagements near Kharkiv, including a reactive UAV heading toward Kharkiv from the north (Source: @kpszsu/66389).

Russian attacks on Zaporizhzhia gas stations and a substation in the Zaporozhye region indicate continued pressure on eastern energy infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31154; @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian reports of a reactive Shahed UAV approaching Poltava from the north suggest expanded UAV activity in the eastern sector (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68050).
Ukrainian drone activity was noted near Starij Saltyv in Kharkiv region, moving southwest (Source: @kpszsu/66356).
High alert issued for possible Iskander‑M launches from Bryansk region, indicating a potential threat to northern Ukrainian territories (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68037).
Ukrainian reconnaissance reported reactive UAVs crossing from Bryansk toward Klimovo, suggesting increased Russian aerial reconnaissance from the north (Source: @eRadarrua/98156).
Ukrainian forces reported engagements on the Kharkiv front, with assault units fighting in villages such as Losevka and Ukrainka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian drone interceptions over Kyiv demonstrate defensive activity against northern approaches (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian Southern Defense reported a powerful fire resulting from a strike, forcing Russian occupiers to retreat from positions on the Kinburn Spit (Source: @UkraineNow/82175).
Ukrainian officials noted evacuation of surviving personnel after heavy fire forced Russian forces to abandon defensive lines on the Kinburn Spit (Source: @UkraineNow/82170).
Russian attacks on the southern front included strikes on oil depots and infrastructure in the Poltava region, affecting supply lines (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian forces continue to hold positions in the southern coastal area despite Russian artillery pressure (Source: @UkraineNow/82175).
Russian launch of an Iskander‑M ballistic missile and 90 drones across multiple regions marked a significant escalation in aerial attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down several enemy drones over Kyiv and Crimea during the night, showing active air defense operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian UAVs conducted strikes on Russian logistics targets, including an oil depot in Poltava (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Reports of reactive UAVs heading toward Kharkiv and Poltava from the north indicate ongoing low‑altitude UAV activity on both sides (Source: @kpszsu/66389; @mon1tor_ua/68050).

key_developments: Russian forces launched an Iskander‑M ballistic missile and 90 drones, causing civilian injuries and infrastructure damage in Sumy, Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv regions (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted multiple enemy drones over Kyiv and Crimea, reducing the immediate aerial threat (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian UAVs struck a Russian oil depot in Poltava, igniting a fire and disrupting enemy logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Gas stations in Zaporizhzhia, Sumy and the Nikopol district were hit by Russian strikes, resulting in fires and civilian casualties (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31154).
EU delivered a €3.2 billion tranche to Ukraine for reconstruction and drone procurement, bolstering Ukrainian defense capabilities (Source: @UkraineNow/82171).
High alert issued for possible Iskander‑M launches from Bryansk region, indicating a heightened missile threat to northern Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68037).
French authorities seized the oil tanker Deliver in the Mediterranean, alleging sanctions violations by Russia (Source: @UkraineNow/82172).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian missile and drone launches demonstrate a continued capability to strike deep into Ukrainian territory, targeting civilian infrastructure and energy assets. Ukrainian air defenses have shown effectiveness by downing several drones, yet the volume of attacks suggests a sustained Russian aerial campaign. The seizure of the Deliver tanker indicates expanding Russian logistical pressures beyond the battlefield, while high alert for Iskander‑M launches from Bryansk underscores a persistent missile threat to northern regions.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian forces on the southern front reported successful defensive actions, forcing Russian occupiers to retreat from positions on the Kinburn Spit. In the east, Ukrainian units engaged Russian assault aircraft and reclaimed villages on the Sumy front, while maintaining pressure on logistics hubs in Poltava. Northern and eastern air defenses remain active, intercepting UAVs and monitoring potential missile launches from Russian territory.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of Russian UAV activity is essential, especially reactive Shahed and other low‑altitude drones approaching from the north and east. The pattern of strikes on energy infrastructure suggests a strategic aim to disrupt civilian power supplies, warranting reinforced protection of gas stations and substations. The EU financial infusion will likely accelerate procurement of additional drones, enhancing Ukraine's defensive and offensive UAV capabilities.
